repo_prior_art.cite: failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 pursued proxy-null mediation geometry but did not supply this latent-response completion.

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the general sharp polynomial-program representation for declared discrete SCM assumptions and counterfactual targets.

repo_prior_art.cite: No completed in-catalogue entry gives this endpoint-face formulation for the repaired factorization class.

published_prior_art.cite: It is a compact-feasibility reformulation relative to BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025's no-proxy interval, not yet a demonstrated strict-refinement regime.

repo_prior_art.cite: No active catalogue entry states this response-type reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: Sjolander2009 derives randomized-treatment natural-direct-effect bounds with a confounded intermediate; BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 supplies the current natural-effect comparator.

repo_prior_art.cite: failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 already sought an observable proxy endpoint-exclusion diagnostic, but its bridge-fibre construction was not a valid latent-realizable completion.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source gives a necessary-and-sufficient observable signed-minor/rank certificate and positive rational witness for strict NDE-endpoint exclusion in this exact finite factorization model; GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 instead gives proxy partial-identification methods without this mediator-factorized endpoint question.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: Add DorettiStanghellini2024 (arXiv:2405.13621), a closer binary natural-effect interval-identification result under outcome-related unmeasured confounding, and distinguish its semiparametric cross-world restriction from the proposed proxy-factorized model.

soundness_flags.label: ass:outcome-proxy-incompleteness
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited proximal-mediation completeness conditions are conditional operators involving proxy variation given Z,A,M (or W,A,M), whereas rank of the unconditional map C_W(lambda,qW) is a new measurement-model restriction; retag it novel and justify or replace it by the literature operator.

soundness_flags.label: ass:outcome-proxy-incompleteness plus Open-ended Question 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With K=2, binary W, and the stated factorization, rank C_W<2 forces W to have identical distributions across positive-mass U classes (or reduces to one class), so W is observationally inert; the advertised two-proxy failed-completeness mechanism is not credibly established.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Finite latent measurement model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The at-most-K latent-class cap and conditional-independent measurement factorization are accepted only as a conditional sensitivity model.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any endpoint exclusion is claimed only UNDER the specified finite latent-class proxy-factorization model, with no unrestricted-cardinality or inference claim.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Either reframe K=2 as a one-informative-proxy latent-class restriction, or move the certificate to K>=3 so binary W can remain informative while completeness fails.

Replace the unconditional rank diagnostic with the conditional finite completeness maps used in DukesShpitserTchetgen2023, or explicitly prove why the proposed new operator is the scientifically relevant residual restriction.
Add DorettiStanghellini2024 and state a precise non-subsumption comparison against its binary natural-effect intervals.

Before pursuing a closed signed-minor formula, use the stated elimination handle to produce a positive rational witness with informative proxies, or prove the proposed K=2 regime cannot strictly contract the no-proxy interval for the claimed reason.

recommended_next_step: Revise the failed-completeness model so the proxy restriction is nondegenerate and literature-aligned, add the missed natural-effect comparator, then rerun novelty review.
